channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Inside how podcasters moved the voting needle with young men ahead of Trump's massive win
item.description: President-elect Trump joined his first podcast in 2022 with the Nelk Boys, ahead of joining dozens of podcasts and earning support through an initiative called Send the Vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massachusetts lawmakers push for an effort to ban all tobacco sales over time
item.description: A handful of Massachusetts lawmakers are hoping to persuade their colleagues to support a proposal that would make the state the first to adopt a ban meant to eliminate the use of tobacco products over time.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China releases 3 Americans it imprisoned for years, and Beijing says US returned 4 people to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China arrived back in the United States late Wednesday. Their release, announced earlier by the White House, was the result of a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dementia research and support to continue in Minnesota after Congress passes legislation
item.description: U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar spoke at a senior living facility in Minneapolis about legislation that was passed last week reauthorizing a 2018 act that provides research, education and support for those facing Alzheimer’s and dementia and their caregivers.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Americans' opinion of Republican Party on the rise
item.description: In the poll, 45 percent of Americans said they feel “favorable” toward the Republican Party, up 6 points from an Economist/YouGov poll in late October, when 39 percent said they felt favorable toward GOP.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A number of President-elect Donald Trump 's most prominent Cabinet picks and appointees have been targeted by bomb threats and “swatting attacks," Trump's transition team said Wednesday. The FBI said it was investigating.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump turns to outsider to shake up Navy, but his lack of military experience raises concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump's nominee to be secretary of the Navy, John Phelan, has not served in the military or had a civilian leadership role in the service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio governor signs 'bathroom bill' into law, restricting students from using opposite-sex restrooms
item.description: Republican Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine has signed a bill into law that will prevent schools from allowing students of the opposite sex into restrooms and locker rooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the medical contrarians picked to lead health agencies under Trump and Kennedy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has assembled a team of medical contrarians and health care critics to fulfill an agenda aimed at remaking how the federal government oversees medicines, health programs and nutrition. On Tuesday night, Trump nominated Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota cannabis license lottery challenge moves to state appeals court
item.description: The move comes after a district court judge this week blocked a state lottery to choose which social equity applicants would be pre-licensed to start up cannabis businesses.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Cheap Ozempic? How millions of Americans with obesity may get access to costly weight-loss drugs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of obese Americans would get access to popular weekly injectables that would help them shed pounds quickly if a $ 35 billion proposal from the Biden administration is blessed by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariffs may be targeted at others — but they could impact you too
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump threatened to slap hefty tariffs on China, Canada and Mexico. Those tariffs would hurt America too — if they are carried out.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens to impose sweeping tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China on first day
item.description: The tariffs, if implemented, could dramatically raise prices for American consumers on everything from gas to automobiles to agricultural products.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is promising big cuts. Impoundment is one axe he wants to wield
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump wants to roll back spending that Congress has already approved. But a 1974 law may stand in his way.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump aide sought payment to 'promote' potential picks: Reports
item.description: Trump's attorneys are investigating allegations that one of his top aides offered promotion in exchange for money, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: At the White House, it’s beginning to look a lot like Christmas
item.description: The first lady invited military-connected families to join her on her final turn of decking the White House halls.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts cannabis license lottery that is precursor to Minnesota marijuana retail launch
item.description: A Ramsey County judge blocked a regulatory agency from holding a license pre-approval lottery after applicants who were denied entry sued the Office of Cannabis Management over the process.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Florida officials allege 'pervasive' discrimination against Trump supporters at FEMA
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody and U.S. Rep. Vern Buchanan will hold a news conference Friday "to discuss their efforts to uncover evidence of political discrimination at FEMA and demand accountability," according to a release from Buchanan's office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
